lists.title: Catalan A1 Grammar Path
lists.description: A1 grammar in communicative order: pronouns (and when to drop them), noun gender and number, both article families, ser and tenir, the conjugation families, negation, hi ha, questions, and adjective agreement.

points.title: Subject pronouns (jo, tu, ell…)
points.function: Say who you are talking about — when it needs saying
points.explanation: Jo (I), tu (you, informal), ell/ella (he/she), vostè (you, formal), nosaltres (we), vosaltres (you all), ells/elles (they). The verb ending already names the person, so Catalan usually drops the pronoun — parlo català — and uses it only for emphasis or contrast.
points.culture_note: Vostè takes third-person verb forms, like Spanish usted — but Catalans reach for it less often than Spaniards do.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I am from Barcelona.
You are my friend.
She is my sister.
We are students.
They are from Girona.
You (all) are very kind.
He is my brother.
They are my friends (all women).
I speak a little Catalan.
Do you live near here?
He works in a bank.
She sings very well.
We live in Girona.
Do you (all) speak English?
They play basketball.
They (f.) arrive tomorrow.

points.title: Gender and number of nouns
points.function: Name people and things, one or many
points.explanation:
Nouns are masculine or feminine; feminines often end in -a (casa), masculines in a consonant or -e (llibre, gat).

Plural adds -s, with -a becoming -es (casa → cases).

Watch the spelling shifts that keep pronunciation: plaça → places.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I have two books.
The houses are beautiful.
I have three sisters.
The gardens are big.
I have two cats at home.
The chairs are new.

points.title: Definite articles (el / la / els / les)
points.function: Point to a specific thing
points.explanation:
El (masculine), la (feminine), els and les in the plural. Before a vowel both singulars become l' (l'amic, l'escola).

Catalan even puts an article before people's names in speech: la Maria, en Joan.
points.culture_note: The 'personal article' (en/na or el/la before names) is a very Catalan touch — la Núria arriba demà.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: The house is big.
The book is new.
The children are playing outside.
The coffee is hot.
The door is open.
The girls are nice.
The cats sleep on the sofa.
The windows are open.

points.title: Indefinite articles (un / una)
points.function: Mention something for the first time
points.explanation:
Un for masculine (un gos), una for feminine (una casa). The plurals uns/unes mean 'some': uns amics.

No elision here — una idea keeps its article whole.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I have a dog.
It is a big house.
He has an idea.
It is a good book.
I have a question.
It is a beautiful day.

points.title: Ser — present (soc, ets, és…)
points.function: Say who or what someone is
points.explanation:
Ser (to be): soc, ets, és, som, sou, són.

Since the 2016 spelling reform, soc has no accent — but és and són keep theirs to stay distinct from other words. Professions take no article: ella és professora.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I am a student.
You are very tall.
She is a teacher.
We are friends.
Are you (all) Catalan?
They are at home.
I am from Tarragona.
Are you a doctor?
My father is a teacher.
We are brothers.
Are you (all) from Lleida?
My parents are from Valencia.

points.title: Tenir — present (tinc gana)
points.function: Say what you have — and how you feel
points.explanation:
Tenir (to have): tinc, tens, té, tenim, teniu, tenen.

Catalan HAS its states: tenir gana (be hungry), tenir set (thirsty), tenir raó (be right), tenir vint anys (be twenty).

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I am twenty years old.
Are you hungry?
She has a cat.
We have a question.
You (all) are right.
They are thirsty.
I have two sisters.
Do you have a pen?
My brother has a dog.
We have time today.
Do you (all) have children?
They have a big house.

points.title: Present tense of -ar verbs
points.function: Talk about what people do
points.explanation:
The biggest family.

Drop -ar, add: -o, -es, -a, -em, -eu, -en (parlar → parlo, parles, parla, parlem, parleu, parlen).

The ending names the person, so the pronoun usually stays home.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: We speak Catalan.
She works in an office.
Do you listen to music every day?
They play football.
Do you (all) study Catalan?
I buy the bread.
I work in a hospital.
What time do you arrive?
He prepares dinner.
We listen to the radio.
Do you (all) study French at school?
The children play in the park.

points.title: -re and -ir verbs (perdre, dormir, llegir)
points.function: Sleep, read, hear — the other families
points.explanation:
-re verbs: perdre → perdo, perds, perd, perdem, perdeu, perden.

-ir verbs come plain (dormir → dormo) or with -eix- in the singular and third plural (llegir → llegeixo, llegeixes, llegeix… but llegim, llegiu).

Preferir takes -eix- too.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I sleep a lot.
We are writing a letter.
She reads the newspaper.
Do you hear the music?
Do you (all) prefer a cake?
I have coffee every morning.
They miss the train.
Are you having coffee?
He sleeps until late.
We are missing the train.
Do you (all) read the newspaper?
They write many letters.

points.title: Negation with no
points.function: Say what is not so
points.explanation: No goes directly before the verb: no parlo anglès, no tinc temps. Emphatic denial can add pas after the verb (no vindrà pas), a very Catalan flourish you'll hear more than need.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I don't speak English.
He doesn't work on Sundays.
We are not tired.
She doesn't eat meat.
Aren't you eating?
You (all) don't dance?

points.title: Hi ha — there is / there are
points.function: Say what exists or is available
points.explanation:
Hi ha covers 'there is' and 'there are' — invariable: hi ha un llibre, hi ha dos cafès. Negative: no hi ha pa.

The little hi is the same locative pronoun that makes Catalan Catalan.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.sentence: Hi {{answer}} un llibre sobre la taula.
drills.translation: There is a book on the table.
drills.hint: the verb of the fixed phrase

drills.translation: There are two cafés on the street.
There is no milk.
drills.hint: the verb of the fixed phrase

drills.translation: There is a cat in the garden.
What is there for dinner?
drills.hint: the verb of the fixed phrase

drills.translation: There are no problems.

points.title: Question words (on, com, què…)
points.function: Ask about places, people, time, and reasons
points.explanation: On (where), com (how), què (what), quan (when), per què (why), quant/quants (how much/many), qui (who). Question word first, verb next: On vius?

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Where do you live?
What is your name?
When is your birthday?
What are you watching?
How many brothers do you have?
Who is your teacher?
Why aren't you coming to the party?
drills.hint: why — asking for a reason (two words)

points.title: Adjective agreement
points.function: Describe people and things correctly
points.explanation:
Four-form adjectives follow the noun pattern: nou, nova, nous, noves.

Some have one singular form for both genders (amable, gran). Adjectives usually FOLLOW the noun: una casa blanca.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: The house is white.
The books are new.
My sister is very kind.
The coffee is hot.
The flowers are pretty.
The film is interesting.
The sky is blue.
The door is open.
The plates are clean.
The houses are small.

points.title: The present perfect (el perfet)
points.function: Talk about what has happened (today, recently)
points.explanation:
The perfet = the present of haver + the past participle: he menjat 'I have eaten'.

Participles: -ar → -at, -re/-er → -ut, -ir → -it. haver is conjugated (he, has, ha, hem, heu, han).

Catalan uses the perfet for a recent or today's action, and the periphrastic past for earlier ones.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Today I ate at the restaurant.
This morning I drank a coffee.
Have you finished your homework?
Have you seen a film today?
He lost his keys.
She sang a beautiful song.
We visited the museum.
This week we worked a lot.
Have you already booked the table?
Did you (pl.) have dinner early today?
The children played in the garden.
My friends arrived late.

points.title: The past (passat perifràstic: vaig anar)
points.function: Talk about completed past actions
points.explanation:
Catalan's most characteristic tense: the past is built with the auxiliary 'vaig, vas, va, vam, vau, van' + the INFINITIVE, not a participle — vaig menjar 'I ate', va anar 'he went'.

It's the normal past for earlier actions, unlike Spanish or French.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Yesterday I went to the cinema.

drills.sentence: La setmana passada {{answer}} veure els avis.
drills.translation: Last week I went to see my grandparents.
Did you arrive late yesterday?
When were you born?
He missed the train.
She sang very well.
We visited the museum.

drills.sentence: L'estiu passat {{answer}} anar a la platja.
drills.translation: Last summer we went to the beach.
Did you (pl.) leave the party early?
When did you (pl.) come back from holiday?
The guests arrived at noon.

drills.sentence: Els nens {{answer}} jugar tot el matí.
drills.translation: The children played all morning.

points.title: The imperfect (l'imperfet)
points.function: Describe ongoing or habitual past actions
points.explanation:
The imperfet describes a past that was ongoing, repeated, or a background — English 'was …-ing' / 'used to'. Endings: -ava/-ia (menjava 'I was eating', dormia 'he used to sleep'). ser is irregular (era).

Contrast with the periphrastic past, which reports one completed act.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Every summer we used to go to the beach.
When I was little, I used to read a lot.
What were you doing when I called you?

drills.sentence: El meu avi {{answer}} contes cada nit.
drills.translation: My grandfather used to tell stories every night.
The children were playing in the park.
We used to live in a small flat.

points.title: The future (el futur)
points.function: Talk about what will happen
points.explanation:
The future adds -é, -às, -à, -em, -eu, -an to the infinitive: menjaré 'I will eat', dormirà 'he will sleep'.

Irregular stems: ser → ser-, tenir → tindr-, anar → anir-/anar-, fer → far-, venir → vindr-.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Tomorrow I will go to work early.
Tonight we will watch a film together.
Will you come with us on the trip?
Anna will buy the gift tomorrow.

drills.sentence: L'any que ve {{answer}} català cada dia.
drills.translation: Next year I will study Catalan every day.

drills.sentence: Els meus amics {{answer}} a la festa.
drills.translation: My friends will be at the party.

points.title: Weak pronouns — direct object (el, la, els, les)
points.function: Replace the object with a weak pronoun
points.explanation:
A known direct object becomes a weak pronoun before the verb: el (him/it m.), la (her/it f.), els (them m.), les (them f.), em, et, ens, us.

El veig 'I see him'. Before a vowel el/la elide to l': l'he vist.

After a verb they attach with a hyphen: mira'l 'look at him'.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.sentence: Coneixes en Marc? Sí, {{answer}} conec bé.
drills.translation: Do you know Marc? Yes, I know him well.

drills.sentence: Coneixes aquesta noia? No, no {{answer}} conec.
drills.translation: Do you know this girl? No, I don't know her.

drills.sentence: Aquests llibres són bons; {{answer}} llegeixo ara.
drills.translation: These books are good; I'm reading them now.
If you come, I'll wait for you at the station.
We're here; can you hear us?
The apples? I buy them at the market.

points.title: Weak pronouns — en and hi
points.function: Replace a quantity/'of it' and a place
points.explanation:
en replaces de + something or a quantity: en vull 'I want some', en tinc tres 'I have three of them'. hi replaces a place or a + a thing: hi vaig 'I go there', no hi penso 'I don't think about it'.

Both are very common and often untranslated in English.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Do you want coffee? Yes, I'd like some.
Apples? I've bought a kilo of them.

drills.sentence: Vas a Girona? Sí, {{answer}} vaig demà.
drills.translation: Are you going to Girona? Yes, I'm going there tomorrow.

drills.sentence: Que has estat mai a París? Sí, {{answer}} he estat.
drills.translation: Have you ever been to Paris? Yes, I've been there.
How many siblings do you have? I have two.
Are you thinking about the exam? Yes, I'm thinking about it.

points.title: Comparative & superlative (més… que, el més)
points.function: Compare things
points.explanation:
The comparative uses més / menys + adjective + que 'than': més gran que 'bigger than'. The superlative adds the article: el més gran 'the biggest'.

Irregulars: bo → millor 'better', dolent → pitjor 'worse'.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Barcelona is bigger than Girona.

drills.sentence: És el {{answer}} bon cafè de la ciutat.
drills.translation: It's the best café in town.

drills.sentence: El català és {{answer}} fàcil que l'alemany per a mi.
drills.translation: Catalan is easier than German for me.
This cake is better than the other one.

drills.sentence: A l'estiu els dies són {{answer}} llargs.
drills.translation: In summer the days are longer.

drills.sentence: La salut és {{answer}} important que els diners.
drills.translation: Health is more important than money.

points.title: Reflexive verbs (rentar-se)
points.function: Say the subject acts on itself
points.explanation:
Reflexive verbs carry a weak pronoun matching the subject: em, et, es, ens, us, es, before the verb.

Em rento 'I wash (myself)', es lleva 'he gets up', ens quedem 'we stay'. Before a vowel em/et/es elide (m'aixeco).

Many daily-routine verbs are reflexive.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.sentence: Cada matí {{answer}} llevo a les set.
drills.translation: Every morning I get up at seven.

drills.sentence: A quina hora {{answer}} fiques al llit?
drills.translation: What time do you go to bed?
She feels much better today.
On Sundays we stay home.
Are you (pl.) having fun at the party?

drills.sentence: Els nens {{answer}} renten les mans abans de menjar.
drills.translation: The children wash their hands before eating.

points.title: Contractions (al, del, pel)
points.function: Combine a preposition with the article
points.explanation:
The prepositions a, de, per contract with the masculine articles el/els: a + el = al, a + els = als, de + el = del, de + els = dels, per + el = pel, per + els = pels.

Vaig al cinema 'I go to the cinema'; la casa dels avis 'the grandparents' house'. They never contract with la/les or l'.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I'm going to the cinema tonight.
The grandparents' house is big.
The book is the teacher's.
We walk through the park every afternoon.
I come back from work at six.
We give food to the birds.

points.title: Possessives & demonstratives (el meu, aquest…)
points.function: Say 'my / this' and agree with the noun
points.explanation:
Possessives take the article and agree with the thing owned: el meu llibre, la meva casa, els meus amics, les meves germanes.

Demonstratives: aquest/aquesta 'this', aquell/aquella 'that', agreeing in gender and number.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: My car is new.
Where is your phone?
My friends are arriving tonight.
This book is very interesting.

drills.sentence: {{answer}} casa és a prop del mar.
drills.translation: That house is near the sea.
My sister lives in Lleida.
Our school is very big.
That man over there is my neighbor.

points.title: The subjunctive (el subjuntiu)
points.function: Express opinion, wish, doubt, and emotion
points.explanation:
After verbs of wish, doubt, emotion, or need — vull que, dubto que, m'alegro que, cal que, encara que — Catalan uses the subjunctive.

Present forms: -ar → -i (que jo parli), -re/-er/-ir → -i (que jo perdi, que jo dormi).

Key irregulars: ser (sigui), haver (hagi), fer (faci), anar (vagi).

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I want you to be on time.
You need to do the homework today.
It's important that we pay attention.
Although it's late, we carry on.
I hope you can come tomorrow.
I doubt they are right.

points.title: The conditional (el condicional)
points.function: Say what you would do; be polite
points.explanation: The conditional endings are -ria, -ries, -ria, -ríem, -ríeu, -rien on the future stem: menjaria 'I would eat', vindria 'he would come'. It softens requests (voldria 'I would like', podries 'could you') and states hypotheticals.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I would like a coffee, please.
Could you help me for a moment?

drills.sentence: Ell {{answer}} de gust, però està ocupat.
drills.translation: He would gladly come, but he's busy.
In your place, I would accept the offer.
We would love to live by the sea.
They would be happy to see you.

points.title: Combined weak pronouns (me'l, l'hi, se'n)
points.function: Combine two weak pronouns before the verb
points.explanation:
Catalan is famous for stacking weak pronouns. Indirect + direct: me'l dones 'you give it to me', te'l porto 'I bring it to you'.

The pair li + el becomes l'hi: l'hi dono 'I give it to him'. en combines too: se'n va 'he goes away', me'n dones 'you give me some'.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: The book? Will you give it to me tomorrow?
The gift? I'll bring it to you now.
The parcel? I'll give it to him today.
Apples — will you give me three?

drills.sentence: Ja és tard; ell {{answer}} va a casa.
drills.translation: It's late; he's going home (away).

drills.sentence: La notícia? {{answer}} explico ara mateix. (a ell)
drills.translation: The news? I'll tell it to him right now.

points.title: Relative pronouns (que, qui, què, on)
points.function: Join two clauses into one
points.explanation: que is the all-purpose relative for subject and object: l'home que parla, el llibre que llegeixo. After a preposition, use qui for people and què for things: la persona amb qui parlo, la taula sobre què escric. on means 'where': la ciutat on visc.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.sentence: És l'home {{answer}} viu aquí al costat.
drills.translation: That's the man who lives next door.
The book that I'm reading is gripping.

drills.sentence: La persona amb {{answer}} parlo és la meva tieta.
drills.translation: The person I'm talking to is my aunt.

drills.sentence: La ciutat {{answer}} visc és molt bonica.
drills.translation: The city where I live is beautiful.

drills.sentence: Els amics {{answer}} vénen aquesta nit són simpàtics.
drills.translation: The friends who are coming tonight are nice.

drills.sentence: El motiu pel {{answer}} soc aquí és senzill.
drills.translation: The reason I'm here is simple.

points.title: The imperative (parla! no parlis!)
points.function: Give commands and instructions
points.explanation:
The informal command (tu) has its own form (parla! 'speak!', obre!

'open!'). The polite command (vostè) uses the subjunctive (parli!, obri!).

Negative commands ALWAYS use the subjunctive: no parlis! 'don't speak!', no ho facis!

'don't do it!'.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Open the window, please!
Speak more quietly, the child is sleeping.
Come in, please, have a seat!
Go ahead, I'm listening.
Don't speak so fast!
Don't leave yet, wait a moment!

points.title: The past perfect (el plusquamperfet)
points.function: Set one past action before another
points.explanation:
The plusquamperfet ('had done') = the imperfet of haver + the past participle: havia menjat 'I had eaten', havien marxat 'they had left'. It marks an action completed before another past moment.

Unlike the perfet, it always uses haver (never ser).

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.sentence: Quan vas arribar, jo ja {{answer}} sopat.
drills.translation: When you arrived, I had already had dinner.

drills.sentence: El tren ja {{answer}} marxat quan vam arribar.
drills.translation: The train had already left when we arrived.

drills.sentence: No vaig reconèixer la casa; {{answer}} canviat molt.
drills.translation: I didn't recognize the house; it had changed a lot.

drills.sentence: Ells ja {{answer}} marxat abans de la pluja.
drills.translation: They had already left before the rain.

drills.sentence: Nosaltres ja {{answer}} acabat quan va trucar.
drills.translation: We had already finished when he called.

drills.sentence: Em va dir que {{answer}} oblidat la cita.
drills.translation: She told me she had forgotten the appointment.

points.title: Reported speech (l'estil indirecte)
points.function: Report what someone said or asked
points.explanation:
Report a statement with que: diu que ve 'he says he's coming'. Report a yes/no question with si 'whether': pregunta si véns.

When the reporting verb is past, tenses shift back (present → imperfet, futur → condicional): va dir que vindria.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: He told me (that) he is tired.
He asks me whether I want a coffee.
I think that you are right.
He said he would come the next day.

drills.sentence: No sé {{answer}} la botiga és oberta avui.
drills.translation: I don't know whether the shop is open today.
I asked her to call me.

points.title: The passive voice (ser + participi; es passiva)
points.function: Say an action is done, without the doer
points.explanation:
The passive = ser + past participle, which AGREES with the subject: la carta és escrita, les cases són construïdes. The agent follows per: pintat per Miró.

Everyday Catalan prefers the reflexive passive with es: aquí es ven pa 'bread is sold here', es diu que… 'it is said that…'.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: The letter is written in Catalan.

drills.sentence: Les portes {{answer}} tancades a les nou.
drills.translation: The doors are closed at nine.
Fresh bread is sold here.
It is said that the winter will be cold.

drills.sentence: El quadre va ser pintat {{answer}} un artista famós.
drills.translation: The painting was painted by a famous artist.
Problems are solved with patience.

points.title: Hypotheticals (si + imperfet de subjuntiu)
points.function: Say what would happen if…
points.explanation: For an unreal present condition, si + imperfect subjunctive pairs with the conditional: si tingués temps, vindria 'if I had time, I would come'. (For a real condition it's si + present → futur: si estudies, aprovaràs.) Never put the conditional directly after si.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: If I had time, I would go to the cinema.

drills.sentence: Si fos ric, {{answer}} per tot el món.
drills.translation: If I were rich, I would travel all over the world.

drills.sentence: Si {{answer}} abans, veuríem la sortida del sol.
drills.translation: If we left earlier, we would see the sunrise.
You would pass if you studied more.

drills.sentence: Si {{answer}} bon temps, faríem una passejada.
drills.translation: If the weather were nice, we would take a walk.
If they asked me, I would say yes.

points.title: The gerund (el gerundi: fent)
points.function: Say 'while / by doing' and mark ongoing action
points.explanation:
The gerund ends in -ant (-ar) or -ent (-re/-er/-ir): menjant 'eating', llegint 'reading'. Alone it means 'while / by …-ing': s'aprèn viatjant 'you learn by travelling'.

With estar it makes the progressive: estic menjant 'I'm eating'. (Avoid the gerund of consequence — it's an error.)

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: He fell while running after the bus.
You learn a lot by travelling.
Don't talk while eating!

drills.sentence: En aquest moment estic {{answer}} un llibre.
drills.translation: Right now I'm reading a book.
What are you doing (right now)?
The children are playing in the yard.

points.title: Subjunctive after conjunctions
points.function: Use the subjunctive after 'although, so that, before…'
points.explanation:
Certain conjunctions always take the subjunctive: encara que / per bé que 'although', perquè (purpose) / a fi que 'so that', abans que 'before', a condició que 'provided that', sense que 'without'.

Encara que plogui, sortim 'although it's raining, we go out'.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: Although it's cold, they go out anyway.
I'll explain it so that you can understand.
Let's leave before it starts to rain.
I'll come provided that you're there.

drills.sentence: Per bé que {{answer}} tard, no tinc son.
drills.translation: Although it's late, I'm not sleepy.

drills.sentence: Va marxar sense que jo ho {{answer}}.
drills.translation: He left without my knowing.

points.title: Irregular comparatives (millor, pitjor)
points.function: Compare with 'better' and 'worse'
points.explanation:
The adjective bo → millor 'better' (agrees: millors); dolent → pitjor 'worse'.

The adverb bé → millor 'better'; malament → pitjor 'worse'.

Aquest pastís és millor; ella canta millor que jo.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: This restaurant is better than the other.
She sings better than me.
The situation is worse than before.

drills.sentence: És la {{answer}} pastisseria de la ciutat.
drills.translation: It's the best pastry shop in town.
Today I feel better than yesterday.

drills.sentence: Els seus resultats són {{answer}} del que esperava.
drills.translation: His results are worse than expected.

points.title: Logical connectors (tanmateix, per tant, ja que)
points.function: Link ideas: contrast, cause, consequence
points.explanation:
Formal Catalan links ideas with connectors: per tant / doncs 'so/therefore', tanmateix / però 'however', ja que / atès que 'because/since', a més 'moreover', en canvi 'on the other hand'.

They structure an argument.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: It's raining, so we're staying home.
She is tired; however, she keeps working.

drills.sentence: Ja {{answer}} és tard, anem a dormir.
drills.translation: Since it's late, let's go to sleep.

drills.sentence: No li agrada la ciutat; {{answer}}, hi treballa.
drills.translation: He doesn't like the city; however, he works there.

drills.sentence: El cafè és car; en {{answer}}, el te val poc.
drills.translation: Coffee is expensive; the tea, on the other hand, is cheap.
Given that it's raining, let's take the umbrella.

points.title: Past hypotheticals (condicional passat)
points.function: Say what would have happened
points.explanation:
For an unreal PAST condition, si + pluperfect subjunctive pairs with the past conditional: si ho hagués sabut, hauria vingut 'if I had known, I would have come'.

The past conditional = the conditional of haver + the participle: hauria fet, haurien vingut.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.sentence: Si ho hagués sabut, {{answer}} vingut abans.
drills.translation: If I had known, I would have come earlier.
He would have passed if he had studied.
We would have liked to stay longer.

drills.sentence: Sense la teva ajuda, no {{answer}} acabat mai.
drills.translation: Without your help, I would never have finished.

drills.sentence: Si m'ho haguessis demanat, t'{{answer}} ajudat.
drills.translation: If you had asked me, I would have helped you.

drills.sentence: Què {{answer}} fet tu al meu lloc?
drills.translation: What would you have done in my place?

points.title: Past subjunctive & sequence of tenses
points.function: Match the subjunctive tense to the main verb
points.explanation:
The subjunctive has past forms. The perfect subjunctive (que hagi fet) follows a present main verb about an earlier action: crec que ja hagi menjat.

After a PAST main verb, use the imperfect or pluperfect subjunctive: pensava que vingués / hagués vingut. This is the concordança dels temps.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: I think he has already eaten.
I'm glad you came.
I thought he would come later.
I wanted you to be there with me.
I didn't know they had already left.

drills.sentence: Era possible que ella ho {{answer}} oblidat.
drills.translation: It was possible she had forgotten it.

points.title: The literary past (el passat simple)
points.function: Recognize the simple past of literature
points.explanation:
Beside the everyday periphrastic past, Catalan has a synthetic simple past (passat simple), now mostly literary: -ar → cantà, cantaren; irregulars: ser → fou, fer → féu, dir → digué, venir → vingué.

You mainly need to read it (in older texts and formal prose).

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.translation: The king built a great palace. (narrative)
They entered the dark forest. (narrative)
She was very surprised on seeing him. (narrative)
The hero took his sword and left. (narrative)
Suddenly someone said: 'Stop!'. (narrative)

drills.sentence: Aquella nit hi {{answer}} un gran banquet. (narració)
drills.translation: That night there was a great banquet. (narrative)

points.title: Focus & dislocation (és a Roma que…, el llibre l'he llegit)
points.function: Foreground a word by moving or doubling it
points.explanation: Catalan foregrounds information by clefting — és … que: és a Roma que visc — and, very characteristically, by dislocation: moving an object to the front or end and doubling it with a weak pronoun: el llibre, ja l'he llegit 'the book, I've already read it'; de peres, no en tinc.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.sentence: És a Girona {{answer}} visc, no a Lleida.
drills.translation: It's in Girona that I live, not Lleida.
The book, I've already read it.
As for pears, I don't have any.
Marc, I'll talk to him tomorrow.

drills.sentence: És ell {{answer}} ha preparat el sopar.
drills.translation: It's he who prepared the dinner.
The keys? I have them.

points.title: Formal register & idiom (registre formal)
points.function: Shift between formal/literary and everyday Catalan
points.explanation: Formal Catalan prefers connectors like tanmateix, per consegüent 'consequently', no obstant això 'nevertheless', sempre que 'as long as' (+ subjunctive), and the polite vostè with the subjunctive imperative. Fixed phrases (al meu parer 'in my opinion', en tot cas 'in any case') mark tone.

references.title: GIEC — Gramàtica de la llengua catalana (IEC)

drills.sentence: Al meu {{answer}}, la proposta és vàlida.
drills.translation: In my opinion, the proposal is sound.

drills.sentence: El projecte és complex; {{answer}}, és realitzable.
drills.translation: The project is complex; nevertheless, it's feasible.
As long as you need it, call me.
Nevertheless, he passed the exam.
In any case, see you tomorrow.

drills.sentence: La reunió s'ha ajornat; {{answer}} consegüent, us avisarem.
drills.translation: The meeting has been postponed; consequently, we'll notify you.

points.title: Key irregular presents (faig, puc, vull)
points.function: Use the everyday irregular verbs
points.explanation:
The daily irregulars:

anar (to go): vaig, vas, va, anem, aneu, van — the SAME forms that build the passat perifràstic, so context decides: Vaig a casa (present) vs Vaig anar a casa (past). fer (to do/make): faig, fas, fa, fem, feu, fan. poder (can): puc, pots, pot, podem, podeu, poden. voler (to want): vull, vols, vol, volem, voleu, volen. venir (to come): vinc, véns, ve, venim, veniu, vénen. dir (to say): dic, dius, diu, diem, dieu, diuen.

related.title: The past (passat perifràstic: vaig anar)
related.contrast: vaig + infinitive is the past; vaig alone (or + a + place) is present anar.

drills.translation: I'm going to the market now.
drills.hint: anar — jo; the following words decide present vs past

drills.translation: What are you doing this weekend?
I can't come tonight.
They want to learn Catalan.
She comes by train every day.
We do our homework together.

points.title: Estar — states and location (estic cansat)
points.function: Say how and where things are right now
points.explanation:
estar: estic, estàs, està, estem, esteu, estan.

Use it for temporary states and conditions (Estic cansat, La Maria està malalta) and for where someone is right now (Els nens estan a l'escola). Identity, origin, and permanent qualities stay with ser: Soc de Barcelona. És alta.

estar + gerundi says what's happening right now: Estic llegint.
points.culture_note: Catalan draws the ser/estar line differently from Spanish — location of things often takes ser (El llibre és a la taula), while people right now take estar.

related.title: Ser — present (soc, ets, és…)
related.contrast: ser for identity and origin; estar for states and being somewhere now.

drills.translation: I'm very tired today.
Where are you right now?
Maria is ill.
We are very happy.
The children are at school now.
I'm reading a great book.
